key_insight: Na$_3$Cr(PO$_4$)$_2$ is a distorted $S=3/2$ anisotropic triangular-lattice antiferromagnet that orders magnetically at low temperature and shows a spin-flop transition before full saturation.
engineering_relevance: The work identifies a tunable low-dimensional magnetic lattice with clear field- and temperature-driven phase behavior, useful for designing and benchmarking quantum magnetism models.
method: Single-crystal and powder x-ray diffraction to determine the crystal structure.
Magnetization and heat-capacity measurements to locate ordering, spin-flop, and saturation fields.
$^{31}$P NMR plus ab initio band-structure calculations to confirm commensurate antiferromagnetic order and exchange anisotropy.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a susceptibility maximum near 3.5 K, antiferromagnetic ordering at $T_{\rm N} \simeq 2.6$ K, a spin-flop at $\mu_0H_{\rm SF} \simeq 1.7$ T, and saturation above $\mu_0H_{\rm sat} \simeq 4.5$ T, with NMR line broadening and relaxation peaks supporting the phase transitions.
watch_item: The magnetic interpretation depends on low-temperature measurements and model-based exchange analysis, so microscopic coupling estimates may remain sensitive to sample quality and structural distortion details.
paper_title: Ground-state properties of the $S=3/2$ anisotropic triangular lattice antiferromagnet Na$_3$Cr(PO$_4$)$_2$
